content.title: Your Filler Might Be Moving and Science Finally Proved It
content.preview: New MRI data shows exactly where hyaluronic acid migrates post-injection. Plus: the high-dose olive oil study that has gastroenterologists fighting, and Pat McGrath's reformulated lip balm is not what you think.
content.but_first: Radiologists just published the first large-scale MRI study tracking filler migration in real time, and the images are both vindicating and deeply unsettling.

quote_of_day.text: The mouth is where vanity and vulnerability share a wall.
quote_of_day.attribution: — Dr. Sabrina Shah-Desai, oculoplastic surgeon, in a March 2026 interview with The Face

injection_report.headline: The Filler Migration Study That Changes the Conversation
injection_report.body:
**What's going on:** A team at Utrecht University Medical Centre published MRI findings on March 28 showing hyaluronic acid filler migration in 67% of patients scanned more than 12 months post-injection, with the highest displacement found in nasolabial and lip augmentation sites. The paper, in the Journal of Cosmetic Dermatology, used gadolinium-enhanced imaging to distinguish filler from native tissue for the first time at scale.

**What it means:** This is the first study with imaging rigorous enough to quiet the "migration is a myth" crowd. The data suggests that lower-viscosity HA fillers migrate more readily, and that injection depth matters as much as product choice. Your injector's technique is now a quantifiable variable, not a vibe.

**What's next:** Ask your provider whether they use a cannula or needle for lip work, and which viscosity they prefer. The Utrecht team is publishing a follow-up on enzyme-based reversal efficacy this summer.
injection_report.highlight: The study found that filler placed superficially in the lip vermilion migrated at nearly twice the rate of filler placed in the wet-dry border. Depth of placement is the variable to discuss at your next appointment.
injection_report.related: The full Utrecht MRI migration study with before-and-after imaging comparisons(URL_PLACEHOLDER)

put_it_in_your_mouth.headline: The Olive Oil Dose That Gastroenterologists Are Fighting About
put_it_in_your_mouth.body:
**What's going on:** A randomized controlled trial out of the University of Jaén, published March 29 in Nutrients, found that 50mL of high-polyphenol extra virgin olive oil daily for eight weeks significantly reduced markers of intestinal permeability in adults with metabolic syndrome. That is roughly 3.5 tablespoons, which is more than most people put on a salad in a week.

**What that means:** The dose is the story. Most "Mediterranean diet" studies use 20–25mL. At 50mL you are getting a pharmacological load of oleocanthal and hydroxytyrosol, both of which are anti-inflammatory at the gut lining level. Your skin barrier starts in your intestinal wall, and this study makes that connection measurable.

**This week's cheat meal:** A thick slice of sourdough, good butter, flaky salt, and a shallow pour of the greenest, most peppery EVOO you can find. Functional and indecent.

**What to cut:** Seed-oil salad dressings masquerading as "light" options. If the first ingredient is soybean oil, it is not doing what olive oil does at any dose.
put_it_in_your_mouth.highlight: Look for olive oil with a polyphenol count above 250 mg/kg listed on the label. If the bottle does not list polyphenol content, the producer probably is not testing for it.
put_it_in_your_mouth.cheat_meal: Sourdough toast with salted butter and a generous pour of high-polyphenol EVOO. Three food groups, zero regret.
put_it_in_your_mouth.related: The full Jaén trial on olive oil dosing and intestinal permeability markers(URL_PLACEHOLDER)

lip_lab.headline: Pat McGrath Reformulated Her Lip Fetish Balm and Nobody Announced It
lip_lab.body:
**What's going on:** Eagle-eyed members of the r/MakeupAddiction subreddit flagged on March 28 that Pat McGrath Labs quietly updated the ingredient list on Lip Fetish Divinyl Lip Shine, swapping out the old petroleum-and-lanolin base for a blend of squalane, meadowfoam seed oil, and a ceramide complex. No press release. No relaunch campaign.

**Why it matters:** The old formula was a glossy masterpiece that tanked in the EU market because of lanolin sensitization concerns and updated cosmetic regulations. The new version reads like a treatment product wearing a party dress. Ceramides in a gloss is a real formulation flex.

**The verdict:** Slightly less sticky, more emollient, same mirror-like finish. If you ditched the original because it dried you out by hour three, the reformulation fixes that.
lip_lab.highlight: The new ceramide-squalane base means this is now genuinely occlusive enough to use as an overnight lip treatment. Two jobs, one tube.
lip_lab.related: The Reddit thread that broke down the old vs. new Lip Fetish ingredient lists side by side(URL_PLACEHOLDER)

lips_in_6.text: Galderma filed a new patent on March 27 for a longer-lasting Restylane lip formulation using cross-linked HA with a novel stabilizer. Timeline to market is unclear, but the IP race in lip filler longevity just escalated.
A March 30 preprint in Cell Metabolism found that oral collagen peptides measurably increased dermal collagen density only when taken with vitamin C at the same meal. Timing matters more than brand.
Rare Beauty quietly expanded its Soft Pinch Liquid Blush line on March 29 with two new shades designed specifically for deeper skin tones, after months of community petitions.

Researchers at Seoul National University published March 28 data showing that blue-light exposure from screens reduces lip mucosa hydration by up to 14% over four hours. Your WFH setup is drying your lips.

The EU's SCCS issued a preliminary opinion on March 27 flagging titanium dioxide nanoparticles in lip products as requiring further inhalation-risk assessment. Powder lip products may face new restrictions.

A new Cochrane review published March 30 confirmed that hydroxyapatite toothpaste is non-inferior to fluoride for remineralization in adults, giving the fluoride-free crowd their first robust systematic backing.

rotation_1.text: Tower 28's SOS Lip Treatment is the only thing that fixed the Accutane-adjacent peeling situation on our beauty editor's mouth this winter. Colloidal oat and hypochlorous acid in a lip product sounds weird until it works in two days.

If you are buying EVOO after today's issue, Brightland's Awake is consistently above 300 mg/kg polyphenols and actually lists the number on the bottle. California-grown, harvest-dated, and it tastes like someone crushed green tomatoes and grass in the best way.

quick_hits.label: Best Kiss
quick_hits.text: Zendaya at the Tokyo premiere of her new film on March 29, wearing what appeared to be zero lip product and still making every beauty editor screenshot her mouth. The secret is probably just having lips that good, but the casual flex of a bare lip on a red carpet in 2026 felt genuinely subversive.

quick_hits.label: Keep It Out
quick_hits.text: Cinnamon-flavored lip plumping glosses. A March 28 report from the Contact Dermatitis journal documented a cluster of allergic cheilitis cases linked to cinnamal in over-the-counter lip plumpers sold via TikTok Shop. If it burns, that is not "plumping."

Stanford's dermatology department announced March 30 it is launching a free public database of filler complication case studies with imaging, searchable by product and injection site. It goes live in April and should be bookmarked by anyone who has ever been told "complications are extremely rare."

Seed Health published data on March 27 showing its oral synbiotic reduced self-reported perioral dermatitis symptoms by 31% over 12 weeks in a small pilot. Promising but not proof. Worth watching, not worth switching your whole routine over.

on_our_calendar.text: April 1: Sephora's Spring Savings Event early access opens for Rouge members. The Pat McGrath reformulated Lip Fetish is in the sale for the first time.

April 2: The American Academy of Dermatology's annual meeting kicks off in San Francisco, with a dedicated session on injectable longevity and migration data. Expect a wave of new findings by midweek.
April 3: World Oral Health Day. The FDI is hosting a free virtual panel on the connection between oral microbiome health and systemic inflammation. Registration is open now.

rotation_2.text: Dr. Shereene Idriss's new book Skincare Decoded just landed on March 27 and it is the first derm-authored book we have read that treats the reader like an adult with a PubMed login. The chapter on when to dissolve filler alone is worth the cover price.

Supergoop's Lipscreen SPF 40 remains the only lip sunscreen that does not make us look like we just ate a powdered donut. Sheer, non-gritty, reapplies over color without incident. We keep one in every bag.

last_word.quote: I think the best beauty secret is just being obsessed with something. Passion keeps the blood in your face.
last_word.attribution: — Isabella Rossellini, in a March 2026 interview with Vogue Italia
